ablations.qualitative_note: Slightly less center-heavy than baseline, but still diffuse plaid-like blob.
No qualitative gain; remains blob-like and slightly worse numerically.
Comparable to SBN off, still blob-like but modestly better than learnable saturation.
Slightly below fixed 0.01; no new qualitative improvement.
Worse than fixed 0.01 and close to baseline.

conclusion.takeaway: Rear SBN is not helping this binary-GT setup in its current operating regime. Background-perturbation is better than per-sample-minmax, fixed saturation is slightly better than learnable saturation, and 0.01 is the best fixed saturation_intensity among the tested values.
conclusion.next_recommendation: Use SBN-off as the main binary-GT reference. If SBN must be retained, keep background_perturbation with learnable_saturation=false and saturation_intensity=0.01, then move to the optical-setup axis rather than sweeping SBN further.
